howItWorks.intro: This canada land transfer tax calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Ontario LTT: 0.5% on first $55k, 1% on $55k-$250k, 1.5% on $250k-$400k, 2% on $400k-$2M, 2.5% over $2M
BC PTT: 1% on first $200k, 2% on $200k-$2M, 3% on $2M-$3M, 5% over $3M
Toronto Municipal LTT mirrors Ontario provincial rates (additional charge)
First-time buyer rebates: ON up to $4,000, BC full exemption up to $835,000
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What is land transfer tax?
faq.answer: Land transfer tax (LTT) is a provincial tax paid when you purchase property. Rates vary by province and are based on the purchase price. Ontario, BC, Quebec, Manitoba, and Atlantic provinces all charge LTT. Alberta and Saskatchewan do not have a land transfer tax.

faq.question: Does Toronto charge additional land transfer tax?
faq.answer: Yes. Toronto is the only city in Canada that charges a municipal land transfer tax on top of the provincial LTT. The Toronto municipal LTT roughly mirrors the Ontario provincial rates, effectively doubling the tax for Toronto buyers.

faq.question: What rebates are available for first-time buyers?
faq.answer: Ontario offers up to $4,000 rebate on provincial LTT and $4,475 on Toronto municipal LTT for first-time buyers. BC offers a full exemption on homes up to $835,000. Other provinces may have similar programs.

faq.question: What is the foreign buyer tax?
faq.answer: Ontario charges a 25% Non-Resident Speculation Tax and BC charges a 20% Foreign Buyer Tax on property purchases by non-Canadian citizens or permanent residents in designated areas.

sections.title: What Is the Canada Land Transfer Tax Calculator?
sections.body: The Canada Land Transfer Tax Calculator helps you estimate your tax obligations accurately so you can plan ahead and avoid surprises at filing time. Instead of guessing or spending hours on manual calculations, get accurate results in seconds. Enter your details above and let the calculator do the work.

sections.title: Understanding the Canadian System
sections.body: Tax obligations vary significantly based on your income, deductions, filing status, and jurisdiction. Small miscalculations compound over time and can lead to penalties or missed refund opportunities. Running your numbers through this calculator before filing season gives you a clear picture of where you stand and time to adjust your strategy.
